name: Nothing Ear (2) True Wireless Earbuds

specs.Special Features: Transparent design, ChatSense (call detection), Ear Tip Fit Test

whatWorks: LHDC 5.0 codec on compatible Android devices delivers exceptional audio resolution — the Nothing Ear (2) at $179 CAD sounds comparable to earbuds costing $100 more when paired with a LHDC-capable phone.

Three ANC modes (Light, Maximum, Adaptive) give genuine flexibility — Light is comfortable for extended office use, Maximum handles transit effectively, and Adaptive switches between them automatically based on environment.
The transparent design is distinctive and well-executed — the clear casing shows the internal components in a way that's become Nothing's signature. The case is equally striking.
Ear Tip Fit Test in the app confirms whether the seal is correct before you start using them — a genuinely useful feature that prevents hours of wondering why ANC feels weak.
worthKnowing: LHDC 5.0 is Android-exclusive — iPhone users receive standard AAC and miss the primary audio quality differentiator. Apple users should consider whether $179 is justified without LHDC.
The Nothing X app is required to switch between ANC modes, adjust EQ, and access ChatSense. The earbuds function without it but the experience is significantly reduced.
ANC performance in Maximum mode is competitive but slightly below Jabra Elite 10 and Sony WF-1000XM5 in real-world transit tests — adequate for commuting but not best-in-class.

The transparent design is polarizing — it's unusual enough to generate consistent compliments and consistent criticism in equal measure. Try to see these in person before buying if aesthetics matter to you.

commonComplaints.title: LHDC only on Android, iPhone users…
commonComplaints.body: LHDC only on Android, iPhone users just get AAC — I was disappointed when I realized the premium codec I bought these for doesn't work on my phone.

commonComplaints.title: You need the app for everything…
commonComplaints.body: You need the app for everything — ANC mode switching isn't possible without it, which is annoying when my phone storage is full.

commonComplaints.title: ANC is good but Sony XM5…
commonComplaints.body: ANC is good but Sony XM5 earbuds are meaningfully quieter on the subway for not much more money.

commonComplaints.title: The see-through design is unique but…
commonComplaints.body: The see-through design is unique but my partner thinks they look weird — definitely a love it or hate it aesthetic.

verdict: The Nothing Ear (2) are the best true wireless earbuds in the $150-$200 range for Android users with LHDC-capable phones — the audio quality uplift from that codec is real and measurable. Three-mode ANC is well-implemented, the design is genuinely distinctive, and the Fit Test prevents seal problems before they start. iPhone users should skip these — AAC removes the primary differentiator and Sony or Jabra offer better-balanced value without the codec restriction.

realBuyers.quote: LHDC on my Samsung S24 is genuinely impressive — music sounds more detailed and spacious than my previous $250 earbuds.
I get compliments on these constantly — people always ask what they are and are surprised they're earbuds.
ANC on the commute cuts road noise and background chatter without making me feel isolated — the Adaptive mode nails it.
Best value earbuds under $200 for Android. Apple users should look elsewhere.

metaDescription: Nothing Ear (2) review for Canada: LHDC 5.0 (Android), 3-mode ANC, transparent design, IP54. Honest on iOS codec limitation and ANC vs class leaders. ~$179 CAD.
introText: The Nothing Ear (2) are true wireless earbuds with a distinctive transparent design, LHDC 5.0 high-resolution audio (Android), and three-mode adaptive ANC — offering premium audio performance at a mid-range price for Android users. Available on Amazon.ca (ASIN B0BQFLTR4P) at around $179 CAD.
